question_title: Will the interest in “rihanna” change between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?
summary: The "rihanna" search term spiked to 100 on Mar 9 after a shooting at her Beverly Hills home, then decayed to 22 by Mar 17. The question asks whether the value on Mar 27 will be within ±3 of 22 (band: 19-25). The decay has plateaued at 22-26 over the last 5 days, but the current value sits above the pre-spike baseline average of ~18, creating downward pressure. A key upcoming event—the shooter's arraignment on March 25—could generate renewed media coverage and keep interest elevated. Monte Carlo simulations across multiple models (mean reversion to baseline, stabilization, arraignment bump) give weighted probabilities of ~46% Doesn't change, ~35% Decreases, ~19% Increases. The empirical base rate strongly favors no change (70%), but the trailing regime shows elevated decrease probability (50%) due to post-spike decay dynamics.

factors.description: Empirical change stats for this date range show 70% no-change rate (14/20 days), providing a strong base rate for 'Doesn't change'
Post-spike decay pattern: value dropped from 100 to 22 over 8 days, but has stabilized at 22-26 for the last 5 days (Mar 13-17), suggesting the rapid decay phase is over

Current value (22) is at the top of the pre-spike baseline range (16-22, avg 18.2), suggesting continued downward pressure toward baseline average of ~18, which would push below the 19 threshold for 'Decreases'
Shooter arraignment scheduled for March 25, 2 days before the end date, could generate renewed media coverage and a brief interest spike
Trailing change stats (last 6 days) show 50% decrease rate and 50% no-change rate, with 0% increase rate - recent trend is bearish compared to full-series base rate
The ±3 threshold creates a 7-point 'Doesn't change' band of [19, 25] at value 22, which is moderately wide and absorbs normal fluctuations

reasoning:
**Forecast: "rihanna" Google Trends interest change (Mar 17–27, 2026)**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| **Doesn't change** | **47%** |
| **Decreases** | **35%** |
| **Increases** | **18%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Starting value: 22** (Mar 17), meaning "Doesn't change" requires end value in [19, 25], "Increases" requires >25, "Decreases" requires <19.
- **Post-spike dynamics:** Interest spiked to 100 on Mar 9 after a shooting at Rihanna's home, then decayed rapidly. The last 5 days (22-26) suggest stabilization, but the value remains above the pre-spike baseline average (~18).
- **Downward pressure:** The pre-spike baseline was 16-22 (avg 18.2). If interest continues drifting toward that average, it could cross below 19, triggering "Decreases."
- **Arraignment catalyst:** The shooter's arraignment on Mar 25 could generate renewed coverage 2 days before the end date, providing a potential floor or small bump.
- **Empirical base rates:** 70% no-change across the full series, but trailing 6-day stats show 50% decrease rate, reflecting the ongoing decay regime.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “rihanna” change between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42705)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=rihanna&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=rihanna&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27) for 2026-03-27 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-17. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "rihanna",
  "date": "2026-02-25 2026-03-27",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
